competitive_position.summary: Competing head-to-head with Agiloft (primary, CLM+AI) and potentially Concur (secondary, Expense/AP) in a formal multi-vendor RFP shortlist process. Agiloft is strong in best-of-breed CLM and contract AI; Zycus wedge is end-to-end platform breadth (P2P + CLM + Spend Analytics + Merlin AI in one suite) and total-cost-of-ownership vs. multi-vendor stack. Risk: if legal/contracts stakeholder (Karishma) prioritizes CLM depth over platform integration, Agiloft may win CLM and Zycus may be shortlisted only for P2P, fragmenting the $120K deal. Activation: demonstrate live Merlin AI redlining (not roadmap) to Karishma before July 15 shortlist decision, position Merlin Intake agentic sourcing as differentiation, emphasize integration simplicity and single-vendor support vs. Agiloft CLM + separate P2P tool.

competitors.name: Agiloft
competitors.quote: Agiloft mentioned in vendor-shortlist context during RFP scoping call; Karishma (legal) asked for live AI redlining demo and clause-extraction capability on 2026-06-16, directly mapping to Agiloft's CLM+AI positioning

competitors.name: Concur
competitors.quote: Janice (AP) asked detailed questions about invoice matching, payment workflows, and supplier-portal email support on 2026-04-28, suggesting Russell is running or evaluating SAP Concur for Expense/AP scope

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 2026-02-17, 2026-03-12, 2026-03-26, 2026-04-28, 2026-06-16 (present on all, leading evaluation); SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-24 (champion, vendor-selection July 15); no EB/C-level participant on any call; no Contact Role with Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor designation
champion_strength.summary: Gajendra Badodekar is a strong operational champion: active across all five calls (2026-02-17 to 2026-06-16), owns the vendor-selection timeline (July 15 shortlist target), multi-threaded the evaluation team (legal, AP, finance), and is driving the RFP process forward with clear milestones. However, his sponsorship is at manager/senior-analyst level and he lacks budget-approval authority for a $120K enterprise deal. He has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, CFO, or any VP/C-level executive, and the approval chain beyond his shortlist recommendation is unmapped. His mandate ends at shortlist; he cannot close the commercial decision independently. Champion strength is 'developing' because operational advocacy is strong but executive access and budget-unlocking capability are absent. Risk: if EB is not identified and engaged before July 15 vendor-selection deadline, the deal will stall at the budget gate even if Gajendra shortlists Zycus, and Gajendra's influence cannot overcome that gate alone.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: only Munaf Ansari (Associate Manager), no EB/Executive Sponsor role
Avoma 2026-03-26, Gajendra: 'wrap up vendor selection by mid-July', no budget-approver named
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-24: champion-led vendor-selection deadline July 15, no EB path mentioned
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged across the entire deal cycle. The Avoma call history (5 calls, 2026-02-17 to 2026-06-16) shows no C-level or VP-level participant; the most senior stakeholder visible is Gajendra Badodekar (title unknown but contextually a procurement manager or senior analyst based on his champion role and vendor-selection ownership). Salesforce carries zero Contact Roles with 'Economic Buyer' or 'Executive Sponsor' designation, and the free-text fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) are unavailable in this org or unpopulated. On the 2026-03-26 call Gajendra stated 'I need to wrap up vendor selection by mid-July' and took ownership of the shortlist recommendation, but he did NOT name the final budget approver or describe the approval chain. The Next Step history (2026-06-24) notes 'Champion needs to wrap up vendor selection by July 15' but does not name who approves the commercial decision after shortlisting. With 17 days to close and a $120K enterprise P2P/CLM deal, the absence of EB identification is fatal: Gajendra cannot approve this spend independently, and without a named CFO, CPO, or VP Finance sponsor the deal will stall at the budget gate even if shortlisted on-time. The evidence shows Gajendra controls the evaluation and shortlist process (active sponsorship at his level) but cannot close the commercial loop.

recommended_moves: Map and secure 30-minute intro call with the relevant stakeholder or CPO (the Economic Buyer) by July 7 to present commercial summary, validate ROI, and confirm budget-approval path before July 15 vendor-selection deadline. Champion Gajendra cannot approve $120K independently; EB engagement is the single gate between shortlist and signature.

Deliver live working demo of Merlin AI redlining assistance (or working beta/dev version) to Karishma (legal) and evaluation team by July 3, with screen-share session to validate clause extraction, global standards enforcement, and time-savings vs. manual review. This is the explicit validation Karishma requested 2026-06-16 and is overdue 14 days.

Provide written confirmation of open-source AI integration support (including SDK/API access, documentation, and example use cases for custom agent development) to evaluation team by July 3. Stakeholder asked this question 2026-06-16 and answer is not on record; it is a strategic AI decision criterion for Russell.
